compositions.composition_note: Equiatomic HEA with lattice constant a0 = 3.552 Å from EAM potential
compositions.processing_conditions: original: Molecular dynamics (MD) simulations using LAMMPS; EAM potential by Farkas and Caro; Random HEA model built by dividing cell into ~4 nm cuboidal cells, each populated with five elements randomly; Energy minimization via nonlinear conjugate gradient method; Nosé-Hoover thermostat and barostat for temperature equilibration; Simulation cell pre-strained in Y direction via iterative process to relieve thermal expansion stress || simplified: MD simulations using LAMMPS with Farkas-Caro EAM potential; random HEA model construction with ~4 nm cell granularity; energy minimization and Nosé-Hoover equilibration
compositions.equipment: LAMMPS; OVITO for visualization; DXA for dislocation identification
compositions.microstructure_description: original: FCC single-phase solid solution; dislocation cores split into Shockley partial dislocations with connecting stacking fault; significant waviness observed in dislocation core structure due to variable local chemical and energetic environment; potential energy and intrinsic SFE fluctuate locally on the (111) slip plane; screw and edge dislocations exhibit different correlation lengths (screw ~3.2 nm, edge ~5.4 nm at 300 K) || simplified: FCC solid solution with wavy dislocation cores; partial dislocation splitting with stacking fault; pronounced core waviness from local energy variations

characterisation.MD_Simulation: LAMMPS with Farkas-Caro EAM potential for FeNiCrCoCu HEA
characterisation.DXA: Dislocation extraction algorithm in OVITO for dislocation identification
characterisation.Correlation_Function: Correlation length analysis for dislocation waviness characterization
characterisation.Waviness_Measurement: Standard deviation of position of Shockley partial dislocation segments along core direction

properties_of_composition.measurement_condition: 300 K; phonon drag regime with tau from 145 MPa upward
properties_of_composition.note: Critical shear stress ~100-110 MPa for continuous motion
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Transition to nonlinear regime at 350-400 MPa

properties_of_composition.measurement_condition: 300 K; phonon drag regime with tau from 145 MPa upward
properties_of_composition.note: Critical shear stress ~125 MPa for continuous motion
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Transition to nonlinear regime at 250-300 MPa

properties_of_composition.measurement_condition: 300 K; lowest critical shear stress ~95 MPa
properties_of_composition.test_specimen: 30° mixed dislocation (60° leading partial in positive motion)
properties_of_composition.note: Steepest slope in phonon drag region; highest transition velocity
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Direction dependence study shows slightly higher velocity in negative direction

properties_of_composition.measurement_condition: 300 K; edge leading partial in positive motion direction
properties_of_composition.note: Similar mobility to edge in linear regime
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Modest directional dependence compared to homogeneous Al/Ni

properties_of_composition.measurement_condition: Phonon drag regime; B/T variation 40% (screw) and 36% (edge) from 300-700K indicating nonlinear temperature dependence
properties_of_composition.note: Nonlinear temperature dependence contrasts with linear behavior in pure Ni

compositions.composition_note: Ni-rich HEA with increased Ni to 30 at.% while keeping Fe,Cr,Co,Cu at 17.5 at.% each; lattice constant a0 = 3.548 Å
compositions.processing_conditions: original: Molecular dynamics (MD) simulations using LAMMPS; EAM potential by Farkas and Caro; Random HEA model built by dividing cell into ~4 nm cuboidal cells, each populated with five elements randomly at specified concentrations; Energy minimization via nonlinear conjugate gradient method; Nosé-Hoover thermostat and barostat for temperature equilibration || simplified: MD simulations using LAMMPS with Farkas-Caro EAM potential; Ni-rich composition (30% Ni); random HEA model construction; energy minimization and Nosé-Hoover equilibration
compositions.equipment: LAMMPS; OVITO for visualization; DXA for dislocation identification
compositions.microstructure_description: original: FCC single-phase solid solution; dislocation cores show high waviness due to variable local chemical environment; dislocation motion studied with four character angles (screw, 30°, 60°, edge); Ni-rich composition shows higher dislocation velocity compared to equiatomic and Cu-rich HEAs || simplified: FCC solid solution with wavy dislocation cores; Ni-rich composition shows enhanced dislocation mobility

characterisation.MD_Simulation: LAMMPS with Farkas-Caro EAM potential for Ni-rich FeNiCrCoCu HEA

properties_of_composition.note: Ni-rich HEA shows higher dislocation velocity than equiatomic and Cu-rich HEAs
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Critical shear stress in range 100-110 MPa for screw dislocation

properties_of_composition.note: Parameters from Table 6 using offset fitting method
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Higher B values than pure Ni; phonon drag regime parameters

compositions.composition_note: Cu-rich HEA with increased Cu to 30 at.% while keeping Fe,Ni,Cr,Co at 17.5 at.% each; lattice constant a0 = 3.560 Å
compositions.processing_conditions: original: Molecular dynamics (MD) simulations using LAMMPS; EAM potential by Farkas and Caro; Random HEA model built by dividing cell into ~4 nm cuboidal cells, each populated with five elements randomly at specified concentrations; Energy minimization via nonlinear conjugate gradient method; Nosé-Hoover thermostat and barostat for temperature equilibration || simplified: MD simulations using LAMMPS with Farkas-Caro EAM potential; Cu-rich composition (30% Cu); random HEA model construction; energy minimization and Nosé-Hoover equilibration
compositions.equipment: LAMMPS; OVITO for visualization; DXA for dislocation identification
compositions.microstructure_description: original: FCC single-phase solid solution; dislocation cores show high waviness due to variable local chemical environment; Cu-rich composition shows lower dislocation velocity compared to Ni-rich HEA in phonon drag regime; at high stresses, velocity similar to or slightly lower than homogeneous Cu in radiative damping regime || simplified: FCC solid solution with wavy dislocation cores; Cu-rich composition shows reduced dislocation mobility compared to Ni-rich HEA

characterisation.MD_Simulation: LAMMPS with Farkas-Caro EAM potential for Cu-rich FeNiCrCoCu HEA

properties_of_composition.note: Cu-rich HEA shows lower velocity than Ni-rich HEA; velocity lower than homogeneous Cu at low stress but higher in radiative damping regime
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Highest phonon drag coefficient among HEAs studied

properties_of_composition.note: Parameters from Table 6 using offset fitting method
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Highest B values among all materials; lowest critical velocity for screw

compositions.composition_note: Pure Ni reference; smallest atom size among HEA elements; lattice constant a0 = 3.521 Å (EAM); intrinsic SFE = 123.5 mJ/m²
compositions.processing_conditions: original: Molecular dynamics (MD) simulations using LAMMPS; EAM potential for Ni (Mishin et al.); dislocation mobility studied with four character angles; Nosé-Hoover thermostat and barostat for temperature equilibration; constant force applied parallel to Burgers vector on top boundary || simplified: MD simulations using LAMMPS with Mishin EAM potential; pure FCC Ni reference; dislocation mobility at 300, 500, 700 K
compositions.equipment: LAMMPS; OVITO for visualization; DXA for dislocation identification
compositions.microstructure_description: original: Pure FCC Ni with dislocation cores splitting into Shockley partial dislocations; minimal waviness in dislocation cores compared to HEAs; dislocation motion occurs at much lower critical shear stress (~5 MPa) compared to HEAs (~100 MPa); edge dislocation velocity always higher than screw velocity (consistent with homogeneous FCC metals) || simplified: Pure FCC Ni with planar dislocation cores; much lower critical shear stress than HEAs; edge dislocations more mobile than screw dislocations

characterisation.MD_Simulation: LAMMPS with Mishin EAM potential for pure Ni

properties_of_composition.value: Linear relationship with tau/T up to 0.3 MPa/K; See mobility curves in Figure 6,8,9
properties_of_composition.measurement_condition: 300 K; critical shear stress ~5 MPa; linear regime up to ~75-125 MPa (screw) or ~175-200 MPa (edge)
properties_of_composition.note: Linear B/T dependence on temperature (consistent with Leibfried model); dislocation velocity always higher than in HEAs
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Transition to nonlinear regime at lower stresses than HEAs

properties_of_composition.measurement_condition: Phonon drag regime; linear temperature dependence; relative variation ~16% from 300-700K
properties_of_composition.note: Linear temperature dependence consistent with Leibfried model for phonon damping
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Reference for comparison with HEA nonlinear behavior

properties_of_composition.note: Parameters from Table 5 and 6 using offset fitting method; lowest B values among all materials

compositions.composition_note: Pure Cu reference; largest atom size among HEA elements; lattice constant a0 = 3.621 Å (EAM); intrinsic SFE = 43.2 mJ/m²; SFE similar to HEAs
compositions.processing_conditions: original: Molecular dynamics (MD) simulations using LAMMPS; EAM potential for Cu; dislocation mobility studied with four character angles; Nosé-Hoover thermostat and barostat for temperature equilibration; constant force applied parallel to Burgers vector on top boundary || simplified: MD simulations using LAMMPS with Cu EAM potential; pure FCC Cu reference; dislocation mobility at 300 K
compositions.equipment: LAMMPS; OVITO for visualization; DXA for dislocation identification
compositions.microstructure_description: original: Pure FCC Cu with dislocation cores splitting into Shockley partial dislocations; minimal waviness; critical shear stress ~5 MPa; dislocation velocity higher than HEAs at low stress but lower in radiative damping regime; SFE similar to HEAs explaining some similarities in behavior; edge dislocation velocity always higher than screw velocity || simplified: Pure FCC Cu with planar dislocation cores; similar SFE to HEAs; edge dislocations more mobile than screw dislocations

properties_of_composition.measurement_condition: 300 K; critical shear stress ~5 MPa; velocity higher than HEAs at low stress, lower in radiative regime
properties_of_composition.note: Cu velocity higher than HEAs at low stress; SFE similar to HEAs leads to some behavioral similarities
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Lowest critical velocity for screw dislocation (0.433 nm/ps)

properties_of_composition.note: Parameters from Table 6 using offset fitting method
properties_of_composition.additional_information: High B values comparable to Cu-rich HEA; low critical velocity for screw
